message:
style: rustfmt formatting pass and minor idiomatic improvements

Apply rustfmt-consistent formatting across the codebase after the
0.2.28 version bump:

- reconstruct.rs, messaging.rs: break long if-condition chains across
  multiple lines for readability
- guard/lib.rs: collapse single-line assert messages, normalize trailing
  whitespace and method chaining in test fixtures
- console.rs: replace closure-based char search with array pattern in
  find() for URL authority parsing
- server/lib.rs: extract HealthCountCacheValue type alias for the
  LazyLock static; replace match-with-None-arm on DispatchPermit with
  let-else for cleaner control flow
- llm.rs: join single-line or_else chains; break long test string
  across lines
- products.rs: collapse multi-line write! macro into single line
- reservation_index.rs: expand terse assert messages into multi-line
  assert_eq with descriptive labels
- resources.rs: break long let binding across lines
- messaging_error_parity.rs: align trailing comments on None/Some
  arguments for visual clarity

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -229,17 +229,17 @@ fn test_invalid_importance_error() {
             vec!["RedPeak".to_string()],
             "Test subject".to_string(),
             "Test body".to_string(),

-            None, // ack_required
-            None, // thread_id
-            None, // topic
-            None, // broadcast
-            None, // auto_contact_if_blocked

+            None,                              // ack_required
+            None,                              // thread_id
+            None,                              // topic
+            None,                              // broadcast
+            None,                              // auto_contact_if_blocked
+            None,                              // sender_token
         )
         .await
         .expect_err("invalid importance should fail");
@@ -424,17 +424,17 @@ fn test_broadcast_with_explicit_to_error() {
             vec!["RedPeak".to_string()],
             "Test subject".to_string(),
             "Test body".to_string(),
-            None, // cc
-            None, // bcc
-            None, // attachment_paths
-            None, // convert_images
-            None, // importance
-            None, // ack_required
-            None, // thread_id
-            None, // topic
+            None,       // cc
+            None,       // bcc
+            None,       // attachment_paths
+            None,       // convert_images
+            None,       // importance
+            None,       // ack_required
+            None,       // thread_id
+            None,       // topic
             Some(true), // broadcast
-            None, // auto_contact_if_blocked
-            None, // sender_token
+            None,       // auto_contact_if_blocked
+            None,       // sender_token
         )
         .await
         .expect_err("broadcast + explicit to should fail");
